purpose: Explains why this cycle's inflation is structurally slower to resolve than historical episodes by mapping impaired monetary transmission channels
components: Mortgage lock-in effect freezing housing turnover and insulating household balance sheets
Equity wealth channel operating in reverse (amplifying demand)
Covenant-lite credit structures (>90%) delaying default channel by 12-24 months
CLO plumbing reducing marginal credit market discipline
Fiscal channel (6-7% GDP deficits) partially offsetting tightening
Aggregate: 525bp hikes producing only ~100bp FCI tightening
current_assessment: Multiple transmission channels impaired simultaneously, creating a historically distinct regime. Services inflation is a labor cost phenomenon (NKPC) operating through an impaired transmission mechanism (rate channel) with delayed credit-channel feedback (covenant-lite). Whether monetary policy can resolve this without recession remains the central unresolved question.
limitations: Difficult to separate transmission impairment from the hypothesis that policy simply has not been restrictive enough
Degree of impairment is measured residually with wide confidence intervals
Transmission impairment may self-correct as mortgage lock-in erodes over time
525bp/100bp ratio may overstate impairment if supply normalization did significant independent work
